what_it_does: Defines the application-facing DNA layer for a product/mobile design system: device canvases, screen archetypes, navigation, information architecture, actions, states, flows, reusable components, and a viewer layout model.
observations: Declares mobile-first product ground with device dimensions for mobile, mobile_landscape, tablet, and desktop.
Separates evidence into deck-direct, product-direct, corpus-direct, and derived-from-principle records.
Maps design-language concepts onto app concepts: pages to screens, zones to regions, sequences to flows, registers to states, and story-tree to information architecture.
Defines screen archetypes including canvas, sheet, focus, ledger, compare, and dashboard.
Specifies interaction rules such as one gold action per screen, thumb-reach placement, dark-glass navigation chrome, and fragment-self-contained screens.
Models states as register-driven variants for empty, loading, error, and success.
Includes component records for actions, version tabs, rails, search, frosted cards, hotspots, markup tools, level pills, style sheets, sharing, and numbered hotspots.
Defines viewer_layout as a work/panel composition with typed panel fills and split-ratio configurations.
standouts: The file treats application UI as a direct product-scale resolution of a broader design grammar.
Evidence status is embedded throughout records, distinguishing declared constants, product-direct observations, and derived predictions.
The viewer layout is modeled as a dialed split system with typed panel fills rather than separate bespoke layouts.
The component records include stateful render data for controls such as gold_action and version_control.
conventions: Uses prose-heavy JSON records with fields such as is, rule, evidence, status, note, and provenance.
Keeps many values as semantic token addresses rather than literal colors or measurements.
Marks derived records explicitly and frames them as falsifiable when product evidence arrives.
Uses nested objects to encode design rules, component states, and layout behavior.
Names relationships in conceptual terms such as binds, accepts, focal, major, and reflow.
concerns: Several records are intentionally provisional because they are marked derived or awaiting product evidence.
Evidence and status placement varies by section: some entries carry record-level evidence while others rely on parent status or prose notes.
Some fields combine rule text, rationale, provenance, and implementation hints in single prose strings, which may be hard to query structurally.
notes: Brief someone on this as the product/application ledger that translates the design DNA into mobile screens, UI components, flows, states, and viewer composition rules.
questions: Which fields are intended to be machine-resolved versus human-readable briefing text?
Are the derived records expected to remain in this file after product evidence arrives, or be replaced by evidence-backed records?
Should evidence classes have a normalized schema rather than prose strings?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is an application face for the design DNA, and the file actually defines product-ground screens, components, navigation, flows, states, and viewer-layout rules.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make the product UI generatable from design-language records while preserving provenance, evidence status, and falsifiable derived assumptions.
novelty: A design-system JSON file that encodes product UI patterns as a narrative DNA ledger with evidence classes, conceptual laws, renderable component states, and dial-driven layout configurations.
contribution: Contributes the application/product layer: device registry, screen archetypes, UI component records, state language, flow choreography, navigation semantics, information-architecture rules, and the work/panel viewer model that a fused system would keep as product-facing design substrate.
summary_for_embedding: This is a prose-rich JSON ledger defining an application/mobile design-system layer with devices, screen archetypes, navigation, IA, actions, states, flows, components, and viewer layout rules.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to translate a broader design DNA into product UI patterns and renderable application behavior with explicit evidence status.
